title: Quantum criticality and factorization in a constrained Rydberg spin chain
authors: Yuan Jiang, Wen-Long You, Liangsheng Li, Maoxin Liu
abstract: We investigate the zero-temperature phase diagram of a one-dimensional constrained quantum spin chain realized in coherently driven Rydberg-atom arrays with competing local Rabi driving and dipole-dipole exchange interactions. Projecting onto the blockade-constrained Hilbert space yields an effective model in which local and nonlocal quantum fluctuations compete on equal footing. Combining exact diagonalization, the density-matrix renormalization group, and variational uniform matrix-product-state calculations, we establish a complete phase diagram comprising a Luttinger liquid, an antiferromagnetic ordered phase, and a polarized paramagnet. We identify two distinct mechanisms for the destruction of antiferromagnetic order: a conventional Ising transition at strong driving and a continuous quantum melting into the Luttinger liquid at weak driving, characterized using entanglement-based diagnostics and finite-entanglement scaling. In addition, we uncover an exact ground-state factorization line embedded within the ordered phase, providing an analytically tractable zero-entanglement reference point for experiments with programmable Rydberg quantum simulators.
summary: This paper investigates the phase transitions of a 1D Rydberg spin chain subject to a blockade constraint. It identifies a Luttinger liquid, an antiferromagnetic phase, and a paramagnetic phase, and highlights a unique line in parameter space where the many-body ground state becomes a simple product state.
main_problem: Characterizing the zero-temperature phase diagram and quantum criticality of a 1D constrained Rydberg spin chain where local Rabi driving and nonlocal dipolar exchange compete.
main_result: The study establishes a phase diagram containing a Luttinger liquid, an antiferromagnetic phase, and a polarized paramagnet, while discovering an exact ground-state factorization line within the AFM phase.
method: The authors use a combination of Exact Diagonalization (ED), Density Matrix Renormalization Group (DMRG), and Variational Uniform Matrix Product State (vMPS) calculations, alongside Schrieffer-Wolff transformations.
model_or_system: A 1D constrained quantum spin chain realized in Rydberg-atom arrays, featuring a blockade constraint that prevents adjacent excitations and includes competing transverse driving and longitudinal detuning.
key_observables: Entanglement entropy, entanglement-based diagnostics, finite-entanglement scaling, fidelity susceptibility, and density-density correlation functions.
important_parameters: Rabi driving strength (lambda), effective detuning (h), and the Rydberg blockade constraint.
main_assumptions: The long-range van der Waals interactions are truncated to nearest-neighbor terms, and the system is studied in the strong-blockade limit.
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows the energy level scheme and interaction representations; Fig 2 presents the phase diagram with entanglement entropy; Fig 3/4 shows scaling analysis; Fig 5 maps nearest-neighbor concurrence and the factorization line.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the Rydberg-atom-based model, derives an effective Hamiltonian via Schriefer-Wolff transformation, presents the numerical phase diagram, analyzes the two distinct transition mechanisms, and concludes with the derivation of the exact factorization line.
why_interesting: It provides an analytically tractable, zero-entanglement reference point (the factorization line) for programmable Rydberg quantum simulators and explores the interplay between local and nonlocal quantum fluctuations in constrained Hilbert spaces.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) Energy level scheme of a single atom. A mi- crowave field with Rabi frequency Ωand detuning ∆cou- ples two Rydberg states, mapped to a pseudo-spin-1/2 system {| ↓⟩, | ↑⟩}. (b) Diagonal vdW interaction strengths V ↑↑ ij and V ↓↓ ij between atoms at sites i and j. (c) Off-diagonal resonant dipole-dipole coupling strength V ex ij driving the spin exchange process.

FIG. 2. Phase diagram showing the ground state entangle- ment entropy SvN of Heff in the (h, λ) parameter plane for bond dimension M = 20. The distinct entanglement signa- tures delineate three quantum phases: the yellow-green crit- ical LL phase, the dark-shaded blue AFM phase, and the slightly lighter PM phase. The cyan solid curve marks the Ising transition, the yellow line separates the AFM and LL phases, and the white dashed line indicates the factorization line.

FIG. 3. Phase transition and finite-size scaling at h = −0.5. Results are obtained using finite-size DMRG for various sys- tem sizes L and VUMPS for the infinite system with varying bond dimensions M. (a) Fidelity susceptibility per site χF /L as a function of the Rabi drive λ. (b) OAFM as a function of λ demonstrates a continuous vanishing behavior across the transition. (c) Finite-size scaling collapse of the fidelity sus- ceptibility, utilizing the critical parameters λc = 1.55 and ν = 0.97 ± 0.08. The inset shows the power-law scaling of the peak value χm F with system size L on a log-log scale. (d) Corresponding data collapse for the neutral excitation gap, yielding a dynamical...

FIG. 4. Entanglement properties at fixed detuning h = −0.5 for various bond dimensions M. (a) SvN as a function of driving strength λ. (b) Effective central charge c versus λ ex- tracted via finite-entanglement scaling. (c) Magnitude of the derivative |∂λSvN| ≡|dSvN/dλ| versus λ. The inset shows the linear scaling of the peak maxima ln |∂λSvN|m and their positions versus ln(ln M). (d) Scaling collapse of |∂λSvN| fol- lowing the ansatz of Eq. (7), achieved with critical parameters λc = 0.20, ˜ν = 1.00, and ˜γ = 1.74.

FIG. 5. (a) Nearest-neighbor concurrence C(ρ) in the (h, λ) plane, obtained by ED for L = 24. (b) Bloch sphere represen- tation of the single-site state on the active sublattice; points are colored by λ. (c) Concurrence C(ρ) versus system size L at three representative points along the factorization line at h = −3.0 (white dashed curve in (a)). (d) Polar angle θ of the Bloch vector as a function of λ; the solid black curve is the empirical fit θ(λ) = π −2 arctan(λ).
